title: Peter Obi Reacts to INEC Voter Guidelines
summary: Obi comments on new voter registration updates.
details: Peter Obi urges Nigerians to verify their voter registrations and commends efforts to improve electoral transparency ahead of local elections.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Tinubu Administration Evaluates Meter Rollout Progress
details: The Tinubu administration confirms electricity meter installations have reached half the states and aims for nationwide coverage by Q4 2026, boosting power billing accuracy.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Sunday Igboho Court Hearing Postponed Amid Security Review
summary: Hearing rescheduled for later this month.
details: Legal proceedings involving activist Sunday Igboho were postponed due to heightened security assessments, with court officials citing logistical concerns.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: El-Rufai Advocates Broad Education Reforms
details: Nasir El-Rufai emphasized the need for curriculum updates, teacher training programs, and digital education access to modernize Nigeria’s educational system.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Student Loan Disbursement Reaches Universities
details: The federal student loan program completed its monthly disbursements, ensuring students receive critical financial support for school and living expenses.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Kwara Gov Praised for Roads and Infrastructure
details: Religious leaders in Kwara State commended the governor for repairing and constructing roads, upgrading schools, and improving public facilities.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: NDLEA Arrests Ex-Lagos Councillor With Drugs
details: The NDLEA intercepted 40kg of skunk concealed in baby diapers and arrested a former councillor in connection with large-scale drug trafficking.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Transport Fares Rise as Petrol Hits ₦1,200 in Anambra
summary: Commuter costs surge across the state.
details: Petrol prices climbed to ₦1,200 per liter in Anambra, forcing transport unions to raise fares and urging government intervention for relief.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: One Dies, Six Injured in Lagos Road Accident
details: A multiple-vehicle collision on a busy Lagos expressway resulted in one fatality and six injuries. Emergency services urged safer driving practices.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Bandits Kill One, Abduct Five in Sokoto Village
details: Gunmen attacked a rural community in Sokoto, resulting in one death and five abductions. Security forces have launched rescue operations.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Police Arrest Kidnapping Suspects in Edo
details: Edo State Police Command arrested three suspects, including a Nigerien national, connected to multiple kidnapping reports, recovering evidence linked to their network.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Fire Guts Building in Anambra
details: A one-storey residential building in Anambra was destroyed by fire. Firefighters contained the blaze with no casualties reported so far.
author: Editor: Omotara How

title: Two Feared Dead in Adamawa Communal Crisis
details: Communal violence in Adamawa State left two people feared dead and several injured. Government mediators intervened to ease tensions and restore peace.
author: Editor: Omotara How

summary: Venture capital invested in local innovation.
details: Nigeria’s tech startup ecosystem continues to thrive with global investors supporting fintech, health tech, and logistics companies.

details: Lagos State introduced smart traffic lights at key intersections, improving traffic flow and reducing congestion during peak hours.

details: The Ministry of Agriculture provided seeds, fertilizers, and financial support to farmers to mitigate the impact of unpredictable weather on crop yields.

details: The Social Democratic Party called for increased female representation in political offices, citing the need for gender balance and equity in governance.

details: Music star Asake gifted cars to his parents as a gesture of gratitude, prompting discussions on celebrity responsibility and social values.

title: Nollywood Actress Sarah Martins Issues Apology
details: Sarah Martins apologized for the recent roadside cooking clip that went viral, explaining it was part of a promotional shoot, not personal behavior.
